hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity at SSTR2-unique ECL2/TM5 interface (position 5) and introducing charge differentiation at position 11 will improve selectivity margin by strengthening SSTR2 binding and disrupting off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding can be achieved while reducing off-target interactions through targeted mutagenesis. However, structural modeling and docking accuracy must be improved to ensure realistic binding modes.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better structural quality and reduce steric conflicts in the binding pocket, which may improve docking and pLDDT scores.

Focusing on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will allow for targeted mutagenesis to disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
